K_avg_unique growth rate accelerated 4.25x between N=412→428: from 0.004/lesson to 0.017/lesson. Root cause: quality gate (F-QC1, L-298) requiring cross-citations per new lesson shifted the citation density baseline. Recent 16 lessons average 3.13 L-refs each vs 1.6 historical mean. This is ISO-5 (positive feedback) operating on the citation graph: higher K_avg makes cross-referencing easier (more connected lessons = more citation targets), which further raises K_avg.

iso_tags: Classical quality-degradation models assume undirected (random) mutation where changes degrade near-optimal sequences. The swarm's corrections are Lamarckian: each supersession/falsification IMPROVES quality because it's identified through evidence-based review (ISO-10 predict-error-revise loop). The effective error rate is not 10% (correction rate) but the rate of UNCORRECTED errors, which is much lower.

ISO: ISO-19 (replication-mutation — Lamarckian correction as third mode beyond fidelity and variation); ISO-4 (regime change — quality degradation threshold is a phantom boundary for directed systems); ISO-10 (predict-error-revise — the mechanism that makes correction Lamarckian)
